title: Logistics Manager
description: A Logistics Manager in India is responsible for overseeing the movement and storage of goods, products, and resources from one place to another. They plan, coordinate, and execute logistics operations to ensure timely delivery, minimize costs, and maximize efficiency. They work closely with suppliers, manufacturers, and customers to ensure smooth supply chain management and resolve any issues that may arise during the logistics process.
degree_name: Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) in Logistics and Supply Chain Management
top_govt_colleges: Indian Institute of Logistics and Supply Chain Management (IILSCM)
top_private_colleges: Indian Institute of Logistics and Supply Chain Management (IILSCM)

career_progression.year_0_2: Entry-level positions in logistics companies, handling day-to-day operations and coordinating with suppliers and customers.
career_progression.year_3_5: Assistant Manager roles, overseeing teams and implementing logistics strategies to improve efficiency and reduce costs.
career_progression.year_6_10: Senior Manager positions, leading logistics teams and developing strategic plans to drive business growth.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Starting a logistics company or a supply chain consulting firm, leveraging industry expertise and networks.
career_progression.global_pathway: Opportunities in international logistics companies, particularly in the Middle East, Europe, and the Americas, with a focus on global supply chain management.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Challenging to find a job, but opportunities exist in major cities and with large logistics companies.
common_misconceptions: Logistics managers are not involved in strategic decision-making.
